name: Analyse Session Tokens
description: Examine session cookies, JWT tokens, and OAuth tokens for signs of theft, tampering, or replay

sections.title: Procedure
sections.body:
1. Collect session tokens from application logs, WAF logs, or proxy captures
2. Identify the token type (session cookie, JWT, OAuth access/refresh token, SAML assertion)
3. For JWT tokens:
   - Decode header and payload (base64)
   - Check issued-at (iat), expiration (exp), and not-before (nbf) claims
   - Verify the signing algorithm matches expected (reject 'none' or algorithm confusion)
   - Check audience (aud) and issuer (iss) claims for tampering
4. For session cookies:
   - Check Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite flags
   - Verify cookie scope (domain, path) is appropriately restrictive
   - Check for predictable session ID patterns
5. For OAuth tokens:
   - Verify token scopes match expected permissions
   - Check token binding (if implemented)
   - Review refresh token usage patterns
6. Compare tokens used by the attacker with legitimately issued tokens
7. Determine the token theft vector (XSS, MITM, malware, log exposure)

sections.title: Tools
sections.body:
- jwt.io: Decode and inspect JWT tokens
- Burp Suite: Proxy > HTTP History — inspect Set-Cookie and Authorization headers
- Browser DevTools: Application > Cookies / Storage
- Python PyJWT: jwt.decode(token, options={'verify_signature': False})
- openssl: Verify token signatures and certificate chains
